Annabelle (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $84,273,813Details
International Box Office $172,583,714
Worldwide Box Office $256,857,527
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,564,153
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,409,000
Total $10,973,153
Further financial details...

Synopsis

John Gordon has found the perfect gift for both his pregnant wife Mia and their unborn child—Annabelle, a beautiful and rare vintage doll in a pure white wedding dress. Unfortunately, the family's delight with the doll is short-lived. On one horrific night, their home is invaded by members of a satanic cult, who violently attack John and Mia. Spilled blood and terror are not all they leave behind. The cultists have conjured an entity, which is so malevolent that nothing they did will compare to the sinister conduit to the damned that is now Annabelle.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$37,134,255 (44.1% of total gross)
Legs:2.27 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$6,500,000 (worldwide box office is 39.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,185 opening theaters/3,215 max. theaters, 4.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $123,367,783

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 3rd, 2014 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
May 7th, 2025 (Special Engagement) by Warner Bros.

October 3rd, 2014 (Wide) (Australia)

August 13th, 2017 (Wide) (South Korea)
July 20th, 2023 (Limited) (Slovakia)
Video Release: January 20th, 2015 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for intense sequences of disturbing violence and terror.
(Rating bulletin 2339, 9/10/2014)
Running Time: 98 minutes
Franchise: The Conjuring, Annabelle
Cast, crew, or production detail: Prequel
Plot point: Collectors and Collectables, Demons, Fugitive / On the Run, Living Toys, New Parents, Possessed, Suicide, Troubled Pregnancy or Miscarriage

Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action

Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

John Gordon has found the perfect gift for both his pregnant wife Mia and their unborn child—Annabelle, a beautiful and rare vintage doll in a pure white wedding dress. Unfortunately, the family's delight with the doll is short-lived. On one horrific night, their home is invaded by members of a satanic cult, who violently attack John and Mia. Spilled blood and terror are not all they leave behind. The cultists have conjured an entity, which is so malevolent that nothing they did will compare to the sinister conduit to the damned that is now Annabelle.
